name: embeddings
context: Generate vector embeddings for text using the OpenAI Embeddings API
info: The Embeddings API converts text into dense vector representations that capture semantic meaning. These vectors enable similarity search, clustering, classification, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). OpenAI offers two embedding models: text-embedding-3-small (fast, cost-effective, 1536 dimensions default) and text-embedding-3-large (higher accuracy, 3072 dimensions default). Both support configurable dimensions via the `dimensions` parameter for storage optimization. The endpoint is `POST /v1/embeddings`. Vectors are typically stored in a vector database like pgvector, Pinecone, or Weaviate for efficient nearest-neighbor retrieval.
body:
# Embeddings

The Embeddings API converts text into numerical vector representations. These vectors capture semantic meaning — similar texts produce similar vectors, enabling search, clustering, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

## Create Embeddings

```javascript
import OpenAI from "openai";

const client = new OpenAI(); // reads OPENAI_API_KEY from env

const response = await client.embeddings.create({
  model: "text-embedding-3-small",
  input: "OpenAI provides powerful AI models.",
});

const embedding = response.data[0].embedding; // float array, e.g. 1536 dimensions
console.log(`Dimensions: ${embedding.length}`);
console.log(`Tokens used: ${response.usage.total_tokens}`);
```

## Models

| Model | Dimensions (default) | Max Input | Relative Cost |
|-------|---------------------|-----------|---------------|
| text-embedding-3-small | 1536 | 8,191 tokens | $ |
| text-embedding-3-large | 3072 | 8,191 tokens | $$$ |

Both models support the `dimensions` parameter to reduce output size while preserving quality:

```javascript
const response = await client.embeddings.create({
  model: "text-embedding-3-large",
  input: "Reduce dimensions for storage efficiency.",
  dimensions: 256, // truncate from 3072 to 256
});
```

See [[openai/models]] for detailed pricing and performance comparison.

## Dimensions

Lower dimensions reduce storage costs and search latency with minimal quality loss:

| Dimensions | Storage per vector | Best For |
|------------|-------------------|----------|
| 256 | 1 KB | Large-scale search, cost-sensitive |
| 512 | 2 KB | Balanced cost/quality |
| 1536 | 6 KB | Default for text-embedding-3-small |
| 3072 | 12 KB | Maximum quality (text-embedding-3-large) |

## Use Cases

- **Semantic search** — embed documents and queries, find nearest neighbors by cosine similarity
- **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)** — embed a knowledge base, retrieve relevant chunks, feed them to [[openai/chat]] as context
- **Classification** — embed labeled examples, classify new inputs by nearest cluster
- **Clustering** — group similar documents without predefined labels
- **Anomaly detection** — flag inputs far from known clusters

## Batch Embedding

Embed multiple texts in a single API call:

```javascript
const response = await client.embeddings.create({
  model: "text-embedding-3-small",
  input: [
    "First document to embed.",
    "Second document to embed.",
    "Third document to embed.",
  ],
});

// response.data is an array of { embedding, index }
const vectors = response.data.map(d => d.embedding);
```

Batch requests are more efficient and reduce API round trips. Maximum batch size is limited by the total token count (8,191 tokens per input text).

## Vector Storage

Store embeddings in a vector database for efficient retrieval:

- **pgvector** — PostgreSQL extension for vector similarity search
- **Pinecone** — managed vector database
- **Weaviate** — open-source vector search engine
- **Chroma** — lightweight embedding database
- **Qdrant** — high-performance vector similarity engine

Cosine similarity is the standard distance metric for OpenAI embeddings.

sections.title: Create embeddings
sections.body: Call `client.embeddings.create()` with a model and input text (string or array of strings). Returns `data[0].embedding` as a float array. The `usage` field reports token consumption. Batch multiple inputs in a single call for efficiency.

sections.title: Embedding models
sections.body: text-embedding-3-small (1536 dims, cost-effective) and text-embedding-3-large (3072 dims, higher accuracy). Both accept up to 8,191 input tokens. Choose small for high-volume applications, large for maximum retrieval quality. See [[openai/models]] for pricing details.

sections.title: Configurable dimensions
sections.body: Pass the `dimensions` parameter to reduce output vector size. The model truncates its native dimensions (1536 or 3072) to the specified size using Matryoshka Representation Learning. Lower dimensions reduce storage and improve search speed with minimal quality degradation.

sections.title: Use cases
sections.body: Semantic search: embed documents and queries, find nearest neighbors. RAG: retrieve relevant context for [[openai/chat]] completions. Classification: embed labeled examples, classify by nearest cluster. Clustering: group similar documents. Anomaly detection: flag outliers from known distributions.

sections.title: Batch embedding
sections.body: Pass an array of strings to `input` to embed multiple texts in one API call. Response `data` contains objects with `embedding` and `index` fields. Batch requests reduce latency and API call overhead. Total tokens across all inputs must stay within rate limits.

input.description: Text or array of texts to convert to embeddings

dimensions.description: Output vector dimensions (truncates from model default)

embedding.description: Dense vector representation of the input text

depends.reason: Requires an OpenAI API key and client configuration
